market_overview.tape: Indices sit near record highs (S&P 500 ~7,537, Nasdaq ~26,121, both green on the day) but leadership is narrow and concentrated in AI/semis. The engine tags the tape 'neutral / range-bound' with XLK, XLV, XLF leading — defensible, though it understates the melt-up. The live risk is a valuation snapback in the high-beta AI cohort these candidates sit in.
market_overview.risk_tone: mixed — cautiously risk-on with a stretched-valuation tail risk
market_overview.earnings_cluster: Heavy megacap earnings land inside these swing windows: GEV Jul 22 (pre-mkt), META + MSFT Jul 29 (post), AAPL Jul 30, PLTR ~early Aug. Event risk dominates the second half of July for this watchlist.
handle_with_care: META — earnings Jul 29 INSIDE the 18-day window; breakout into resistance ('no room')
MSFT — earnings Jul 29 well inside the 42-day window; technical confirmation still 'forming' (15)
AAPL — earnings Jul 30 INSIDE the 15-day window AND engine quality says 'likely trap — skip' (grade D, trap 74)
GEV — counter-trend SHORT into a Strong-Buy name that reports Jul 22 with a strong beat history; knife-catch risk
PLTR — earnings date (~Aug 3–10) sits at the edge of the 20-day window; thin volume on the trigger
Jul 29 — FOMC + META/MSFT earnings same day; size event risk across the AI book.
market_overview.macro: FOMC decision Jul 29 (2pm ET) — hold at 3.5-3.75% expected (4th straight; new chair Warsh) — lands the SAME day as META & MSFT earnings, with AAPL the next morning. A macro+micro collision dominates Jul 29-30; the FOMC is also in-window for NVDA/PLTR.

Steady target raises/reiterations this month; supports the bias but not a dated trigger.
FOMC decision Jul 29 (2pm ET) — hold at 3.5-3.75% expected (4th straight; new chair Warsh, no SEP). A whole-market risk event inside this window.

market_read.note: Engine calls the tape neutral with sector XLC lagging; live tape confirms neutral-to-up but narrow. META is a high-beta AI-adjacent name in a lagging comms sector — it rides QQQ risk more than XLC.

edge_cases.note: Jul 29 print lands before the 18-bar max. If T1 (642.86) fills and you run the T2 tranche, you carry a binary event; consider being flat/reduced before the Jul 28 close.
Breakout into resistance ('no room' quality warning) with the broad tape stretched (BofA snapback flag). A gap-and-fail or a QQQ down-day can skip the 614.52 stop.
Engine: XLC lagging while XLK/XLV/XLF lead — META lacks a sector tailwind even as an AI name.

META.narrative: The engine's read is a clean, high-conviction long: S/R Breakout, verdict TAKE (conviction 100), with-trend continuation, higher-TF bullish, entry 631.48, stop 614.52, target 692.04 (R:R 3.57), size_factor 0.8. The AI AGREES — sentiment is strongly bullish (Strong Buy consensus, ~$827 average target, BofA 'US 1' add) and nothing in the news or tape contradicts direction. The one thing the historical math can't see: earnings on Jul 29 fall inside the 18-day window, so any T2 runner carries a binary event, and the setup is breaking into resistance while the market is stretched. Supportive — hold size and plan to be flat/reduced before the print rather than adding. Deep dive: FOMC (Jul 29, hold expected) lands the SAME day as the print — a rare macro+micro double event; institutions onside (BofA 'US 1'), crowd euphoric (contrarian caution).
META.reconciliation: Technical TAKE and AI both bullish — no divergence. The AI only trims enthusiasm on the runner because of the in-window earnings and the 'no room into resistance' caveat.

edge_cases.note: Despite Grade A / trap_risk 0, confirmation is only 15 ('forming'): still needs to reclaim the last lower-high 466.27, print a rejection/reclaim candle, and show volume ≥1.2×. Price 384.24 is well below that 466 pivot — an early bounce off support 381.88, not a confirmed breakout.
Jul 29 print is squarely inside the 42-day window; an Azure miss or heavier capex could gap through the 375.30 stop before the thesis works.
The active bear case (AI capex $80–146B) has already produced target cuts (Argus). Caps the multiple until Azure reaccelerates.

MSFT.narrative: The engine likes the structure: S/R Breakout, verdict TAKE, Grade A with trap_risk 0, 'room to next level', entry 384.24, stop 375.30, target 436.48 at R:R 5.84, size_factor 1.0. The AI AGREES on direction — analyst consensus is bullish and the sector (XLK) is leading. But stance is CAUTIONARY: confirmation is only 'forming' (15, price still far under the 466.27 lower-high it needs to reclaim), so this is an early bounce, not a confirmed move; and fiscal-Q4 earnings Jul 29 sit inside the window, so the trade must survive a binary print. Take at engine size, but treat it as needing follow-through and manage the event deliberately. Deep dive: same Jul 29 FOMC-plus-earnings collision; XLK leadership helps, the capex debate is the swing.
MSFT.reconciliation: Technical TAKE and AI agree on a bullish MSFT (alignment 'agree'), but the AI downgrades tone to 'cautionary' because the trigger is thin (confirmation 15, below a key lower-high) and a high-impact print falls inside the window. With-trend continuation, so a thin trigger argues for patience, not a fade.

NVDA.narrative: Engine read: S/R Bounce long, verdict WATCH (conviction 92), Grade B / trap_risk 0, entry 202.70, stop 195.58, target 212.15 (R:R 1.33), at support 199.34 with a lower-wick rejection. The AI is SUPPORTIVE and agrees: sentiment is strongly bullish (~85% Buy, ~$302 target), the leading sector is XLK, and — importantly — the next earnings (Aug 26) is OUTSIDE the trade window, so no binary event to manage. The caveat the math flags and the AI seconds: this is a counter-higher-TF bounce (htf bearish) with only 1.33 R:R, so it's a tactical pop that needs the 213.98 reclaim to become more — take the target, don't marry it. Deep dive adds an in-window macro event the setup didn't show: FOMC Jul 29 sits inside the 15-day window even though earnings (Aug 26) do not. Options skew is unusually bullish (calls>puts) but Michael Burry is building bearish AI-chip bets, and export-control policy (H200 China in legal limbo) is a live overhang.
NVDA.reconciliation: Engine WATCH (wait for confirmation) and AI agree; the AI leans supportive because sentiment and sector back the bounce and there's no in-window earnings, while respecting that it fights a bearish higher timeframe.

PLTR.narrative: Engine read: S/R Breakout long, verdict WATCH (conviction 86), entry 129.05, stop 124.59, target 149.85 at an attractive R:R 4.66 — but Grade C, trap_risk 53, and the engine's own note says 'with-trend but low-grade (C/D) for a sized entry.' The AI is CAUTIONARY and agrees to wait: sentiment is only mildly bullish, the breakout is on thin volume (RVOL 0.7×) below the 136.09 lower-high, and earnings likely fall at the edge of the window. The R:R is tempting, but this needs a volume-backed reclaim first; size down (trim) until it confirms. Deep dive: extremely crowded (P/E ~150x, WSB mood flipped bull->bear in two weeks, short interest up to 76M); Burry trimmed his short in late June, which fueled July's +11% bounce. FOMC Jul 29 also sits in the window.
PLTR.reconciliation: Engine WATCH and AI agree it isn't a clean go yet. The AI is more cautious than the headline R:R suggests because participation is thin and a high-impact print may sit inside the window.

AAPL.narrative: This is where the AI pushes back hardest. The engine lists a Squeeze Breakout long (verdict WATCH, entry 316.16, stop 303.50, target 333.99, R:R 1.41) but its OWN quality read is 'likely trap — skip' (Grade D, trap_risk 74) with confirmation entirely ABSENT (0) — it explicitly wants a pullback to 287.38 and a reclaim of 302.42 first. Layer on Jul 30 earnings inside the window and a consensus price target (~$313) basically at spot (~$316), and the risk/reward is poor. Stance CONFLICTING: don't chase this squeeze; wait for the pullback-and-reclaim the engine names, and let earnings pass. Sentiment is only mildly positive and doesn't offset a broken technical picture. Deep dive: FOMC Jul 29 stacks on top of the Jul 30 print — two binary events inside the window with no technical confirmation beneath them.
AAPL.reconciliation: alignment 'agree': the engine did NOT say take (WATCH + quality 'skip'), and the AI concurs it is not actionable. Stance 'conflicting' because the qualitative picture (limited upside vs target, in-window earnings) actively argues against the long here, reinforcing the engine's trap warning.

The AI is CONFLICTING: even setting aside direction, the reward here is negligible (0.19 points) for the risk, and the fundamentals lean the other way — DG just RAISED FY2026 guidance and analysts sit ~14% above spot, so the drift is upward against a short. This is a marginal tactical fade at best; the qualitative picture gives no reason to press it. Skip or keep size minimal and mechanical.

edge_cases.note: klass 'reversal' + COUNTER-TREND + engine alignment 'conflict': per house rules, a counter-trend short is only valid WITH turn confirmation (reclaim of the 856.1 higher-low, volume ≥1.2×) — neither is present, so this is a knife-catch.
Shorting a Strong-Buy name with a strong beat history two weeks before it reports (Jul 22) is fighting both sentiment and an imminent bullish catalyst.

GEV.narrative: Engine read: S/R Bounce SHORT, verdict ARM (conviction 41, size_factor 0.41), alignment 'conflict', COUNTER-TREND against a moderately bullish tape — it explicitly says 'only valid with turn confirmation; size small, target the mean,' arming only on a reclaim of the 856.1 higher-low with volume. The AI strongly CONFLICTS with taking this short: GE Vernova is a consensus Strong Buy (Bernstein $1,206 vs spot ~1,073), expected to grow EPS ~70% YoY with a ~163% average beat, and it reports Jul 22 — just past the window but close enough to matter. Fading resistance at 1094 here, with R:R 0.44 and no turn confirmation, is a knife-catch. Respect the engine's 'arm-only, small-size' framing and, honestly, prefer to stand aside unless a genuine reversal prints. Deep dive makes the short look worse, not better: GEV is +76% YTD on structural AI-power demand, just signed a Microsoft datacenter-power JV, and analysts' own PT (~$1,090) now sits BELOW the price — news, analyst, earnings and positioning are all headwinds to a short. Only a confirmed reversal (the engine's arm conditions) justifies it.
GEV.reconciliation: alignment 'agree': the engine itself did NOT green-light this (ARM, alignment 'conflict', tiny size). The AI agrees and pushes further — the bullish Strong-Buy backdrop and an imminent likely-beat earnings make the short especially dangerous; only a confirmed turn (the engine's arm conditions) would justify it.
